page: Amazon Web Services r6g.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and custom AWS Graviton processors. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture, utilizing the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 microarchitecture running at 2.5 GHz. The processor configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, with hardware support for AES cryptography. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per physical core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, and network connectivity features a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across most multi-core and memory-intensive workloads, alongside some single-threaded performance limitations. The instance achieved a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 48,584.84 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11,647.0, both falling within the average range. Memory bandwidth is robust, with cached small-block read speeds reaching 1,893.45 GB/sec and uncached large-block read and write speeds measured at 165.0 GB/sec and 75.57 GB/sec, respectively. However, single-threaded performance is weaker, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 950.0 and lower-tier results in single-threaded gzip compression and text processing.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r6g.8xlarge offers a high density of memory resources relative to its physical core count, making it a viable option for workloads that require large memory footprints without the premium cost of x86-based memory-optimized instances. Its performance profile makes it suitable for memory-intensive applications such as Redis in-memory databases, where it handles 37.16 million SET operations per second, as well as general database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded data decompression. It is less suited for single-threaded tasks or workloads requiring high-performance local storage.
description: Amazon Web Services r6g.8xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 32 dedicated physical cores, 256.0 GB of RAM (8.0 GB per core), and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps, with no local storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers average multi-core performance, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 11,647.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 48,584.84. Memory bandwidth is strong, reaching 165.0 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads, though single-threaded performance remains a relative bottleneck. Qualitatively, the r6g.8xlarge provides a cost-effective option for memory-heavy workloads due to its high memory-to-core ratio on the energy-efficient Graviton architecture. It is best suited for in-memory databases like Redis, database operations, and multi-threaded processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6g.8xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance with 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 256 GB RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth, suited for in-memory databases and multi-threaded tasks.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6g.8xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 256 GB RAM, and 12 Gbps bandwidth.
tagline: An arm64 memory-optimized instance featuring thirty-two dedicated cores and two hundred fifty-six gigabytes of RAM for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 32 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 arm64 cores at 2.5 GHz
Equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory (8.0 GB per core)
Baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps with no local storage
Delivers average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance in benchmarks
Optimized for in-memory databases, Redis, and multi-threaded workloads
